revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deities = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dardar]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''

{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deities = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deities = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deities = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deities = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

{{Shrine |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == Related Topics == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[673]]–[[686]] (reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)-qq]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

Known for its [[Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[Bingo Province]] to [[Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinai-sha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Fudō Myōō]] and [[Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]
