{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[en:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[Category:Removed translated page templates]]

File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[en:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[Category:Removed translated page templates]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]]

revision.comment: 99 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) up to rev 102030047
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 素盞嗚神社 |画像 = [[画像:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|素盞嗚神社正面]]
鳥居と随神門と拝殿 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[新市町]]大字戸手1-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
（称）[[備後国]][[一宮]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = （伝）[[天武天皇]]年間（[[673年]] - [[686年]]） |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（祇園祭） |神事 = |地図={{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=素盞嗚神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E}}}} }} '''素盞嗚神社'''（すさのおじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]]新市町大字戸手にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[備後国]][[一宮]]を称する。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[祇園信仰]]、祇園祭発祥の神社で、境内は巨旦将来の屋敷跡と伝わる。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * '''[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]'''（すさのをのみこと） ; 配神 * [[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]]（くしなだひめ） - 素盞嗚尊の妃 * [[八王子#宗教|八王子]]（やはしらのみこ） - 素盞嗚尊の御子神 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば[[天武天皇]]の治世であった7世紀ごろ（[[679年]]か）に創建したとされる。その後、[[遣唐使]]であった[[吉備真備]]が[[唐]]から帰国した後の[[天平]]6年 （[[734年]]）に[[備後国|備後]]から素盞嗚命を[[播磨国|播磨]]の[[広峯神社]]に勧請したとされる。 『[[釈日本紀]]』巻7（[[卜部兼方]]、[[鎌倉時代]]中期）に引用された『[[備後国風土記]]』[[逸文]]（[[奈良時代]]か）の「[[蘇民将来]]」説話に「疫隈國社（エノクマノクニツヤシロ）」とあるのが当社とされるが、本来は摂社である「蘇民神社・疱瘡神社」であるとする説もある。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』には「備後國[[深津郡]]一座 須佐能袁能神社」と記載されている。 後に[[神仏習合]]によって仏教系の神である[[牛頭天王]]を祭神とするようになり、「早苗山天竜院天王寺」という真言系の[[別当寺]]が作られた。本堂である本地堂（現 天満宮）には本尊[[本地仏]]として[[聖観世音菩薩]]が祀られた。一般に牛頭天王の本地仏は[[薬師如来]]であるが、当社の観音祭祀の理由は不明である。また脇侍に[[不動明王]]と[[毘沙門天]]が祀られた。「江熊（エノクマ）祇園牛頭天王社」が正式な社名であるが、天王社、祇園社などとも呼ばれるようになった。 [[明治時代|明治]]の[[神仏分離]]により、神社の道を選んだ当社の別当僧は[[還俗]]して[[神官]]となり、祭神は本来の素盞嗚尊に改め、現在の社名に改称した。 == 境内 == ; 本殿 [[ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|本殿]] * [[入母屋造]]檜皮葺。備後福山藩の初代藩主[[水野勝成]]の再建と伝わる。 * 屋根は視線方向の棟の中央で一段高く別棟が直交している。[[千木]]は正面･左・右・奥の4つ、[[鰹木]]は正面と奥の千木の根元にひとつずつ、直交する棟に五つ。建物の平面は大社造りと同じくほぼ正方形。 ; [[幣殿]] : 拝殿と本殿の間に建つ。 ; [[鳴き龍]] : 日光東照宮などで有名で、日本にわずか20棟ほどと言われる。 ; 神代之杉 : かつて境内の鬼門（北東の隅）に立っていた[[神木]]。現在は石碑のみが残る。 {{-}} ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|'''境内''' ファイル:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|'''神楽殿'''
祇園祭にはここに3基の神輿が納められる。[[平成]]10年（[[1998年]]）には石見神楽団がここで当社御祭神素盞嗚尊縁の「をろち」などを奉納した。 ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|'''拝殿'''
入母屋造瓦葺。 ファイル:Tomita-kyuuzaburou.jpg|'''富田久三郎顕彰碑'''
城山から移築された山門横に立つ。[[備後絣]]の開発者である富田久三郎を称える。 ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|'''随神門''' ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 大鳥居.JPG|'''大鳥居'''
境内東端、石造り。江戸時代中期[[元禄時代]]の建立で、柱には建立した別当僧の名が刻まれている。 == 相方城遺構 == 戦国時代に当社の南西にそびえる城山（じょうやま / 標高199m）一帯に築かれていた山城[[相方城]]から、[[城門]]2棟と櫓が移築されたとされる。そのうち櫓は[[1970年]]代の火災で焼失したが、城門は境内の北側を通る奈良時代の旧山陽道に面して現存する。現存する戦国期の山城の[[城門]]としては最古級とされる。[[平成時代|平成]]の大修理では、この城門にも補修が施された。 ファイル:相方城城門.JPG|'''[[相方城]]移築の城門（中門）''' ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 相方城城門 (西方).JPG|'''相方城移築の城門（北門）''' == 摂末社 == ; 蘇民神社と疱瘡神社 [[ファイル:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|蘇民神社と疱瘡神社]] : 本殿の南側に長屋式で建つ。一説では、備後國風土記･逸文の舞台「疫隈國社」とは、素盞嗚神社の本殿のことではなく、摂社であるここが「疫隈國社」であるとされる。なお当社の敷地は、[[武塔神]]（素盞嗚尊）に滅ぼされた弟将来（[[巨旦将来]]）の屋敷跡といわれる。 {{-}} ; 戸手天満宮 [[ファイル:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|戸手天満宮]] : 境内の北西、広場に面して瓦葺き入母屋造りの仏式の建物。元々は当社の「本地堂」で、神仏習合時期の別当寺・早苗山天竜院天王寺の本堂であった。[[江戸時代]]中期の再建とされ、祇園社（素盞鳴命奉祀神社）に残っているのは全国でも唯一である。[[明治維新]]後の[[廃仏毀釈]]の際、祭神に[[菅原道真]]を奉祀して守り通された。その後時を経て、屋根は抜け天井は朽ちて甚だ老朽化が進み倒壊が危惧されたが、[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）[[広島大学]]の三浦教授監修のもと修復がなされ現代に蘇った（平成の大修理）。ここに祀られていた複数の仏像は明治期に周辺の真言寺院に散逸したが、後の調査で、本尊・聖観世音菩薩は、福山市内の某真言寺院に現存していることが判明した。 {{-}} ; 素佐能表神社 : 本殿から大鳥居を抜けて延長線上の5キロメートルほど先（福山市駅家町法成寺）に鎮座。[[長享]]2年（[[1488年]]）、当地の村民が疫病が大流行した際に、新市の素盞嗚神社に日参し、疫病が収まったので、素盞嗚神社の分霊を勧請し祀ったと社伝にあるhttps://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E8%83%BD%E8%A1%A8%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/@34.5573796,133.319335,17z/data=!4m5!3m4!1s0x35511923f416baa7:0xe94ea88b71fa02b0!8m2!3d34.5575979!4d133.3211035?hl=ja。 == 主な祭事 == === 祇園祭 === 祇園祭（現地では「祇園さん」・「天王さん」と呼ぶ）は、福山市の新市（しんいち）地区・戸手（とで）地区と相方（さがた）地区、[[府中市 (広島県)|府中市]]の中須（なかず）地区の4地区の氏子により年に1度、7月に行われる。 現在では戸手と相方が合同となり、3基の神輿で「神輿合わせ（喧嘩神輿）」が行われる。 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === * [[広島県]][[福山市]]新市町大字戸手1-1 === 交通アクセス === ; {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[福塩線]] [[上戸手駅]] （徒歩3分） ; {{underline|バス}} * [[福山駅]]（JR西日本[[山陽新幹線]]ほか）から、[[中国バス]]（府中・[[東城]]方面行き）で、「寺岡記念病院前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車 （下車後徒歩5分） ** ただし、祇園祭最終日19時からは交通規制により、バスの運行ルートが変更されるので注意が必要。 ; {{underline|自家用車}} * 祇園祭期間中は混雑し、自家用車での参拝は極めて困難。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[吉備津神社 (福山市)|吉備津神社]] （福山市新市町宮内） - 一般に備後国一宮とされる神社 * [[祇園信仰]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Susanoo-jinja_(Fukuyama)}} * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 素盞嗚神社]（新市町観光協会ホームページ） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すさのおしんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:福山市の歴史]] [[Category:福山市の建築物]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:一宮|加]] [[Category:備後国|社1すさのお]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:深津郡|社すさのお]] {{Pref-stub|pref=広島県}}

入母屋造瓦葺。 ファイル:Tomita-kyuuzaburou.jpg|'''富田久三郎顕彰碑'''
城山から移築された山門横に立つ。[[備後絣]]の開発者である富田久三郎を称える。 ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|'''随神門''' ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 大鳥居.JPG|'''大鳥居'''
境内東端、石造り。江戸時代中期[[元禄時代]]の建立で、柱には建立した別当僧の名が刻まれている。 == 相方城遺構 == 戦国時代に当社の南西にそびえる城山（じょうやま / 標高199m）一帯に築かれていた山城[[相方城]]から、[[城門]]2棟と櫓が移築されたとされる。そのうち櫓は[[1970年]]代の火災で焼失したが、城門は境内の北側を通る奈良時代の旧山陽道に面して現存する。現存する戦国期の山城の[[城門]]としては最古級とされる。[[平成時代|平成]]の大修理では、この城門にも補修が施された。 ファイル:相方城城門.JPG|'''[[相方城]]移築の城門（中門）''' ファイル:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 相方城城門 (西方).JPG|'''相方城移築の城門（北門）''' == 摂末社 == ; 蘇民神社と疱瘡神社 [[ファイル:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|蘇民神社と疱瘡神社]] : 本殿の南側に長屋式で建つ。一説では、備後國風土記･逸文の舞台「疫隈國社」とは、素盞嗚神社の本殿のことではなく、摂社であるここが「疫隈國社」であるとされる。なお当社の敷地は、[[武塔神]]（素盞嗚尊）に滅ぼされた弟将来（[[巨旦将来]]）の屋敷跡といわれる。 {{-}} ; 戸手天満宮 [[ファイル:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|戸手天満宮]] : 境内の北西、広場に面して瓦葺き入母屋造りの仏式の建物。元々は当社の「本地堂」で、神仏習合時期の別当寺・早苗山天竜院天王寺の本堂であった。[[江戸時代]]中期の再建とされ、祇園社（素盞鳴命奉祀神社）に残っているのは全国でも唯一である。[[明治維新]]後の[[廃仏毀釈]]の際、祭神に[[菅原道真]]を奉祀して守り通された。その後時を経て、屋根は抜け天井は朽ちて甚だ老朽化が進み倒壊が危惧されたが、[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年）[[広島大学]]の三浦教授監修のもと修復がなされ現代に蘇った（平成の大修理）。ここに祀られていた複数の仏像は明治期に周辺の真言寺院に散逸したが、後の調査で、本尊・聖観世音菩薩は、福山市内の某真言寺院に現存していることが判明した。 {{-}} ; 素佐能表神社 : 本殿から大鳥居を抜けて延長線上の5キロメートルほど先（福山市駅家町法成寺）に鎮座。[[長享]]2年（[[1488年]]）、当地の村民が疫病が大流行した際に、新市の素盞嗚神社に日参し、疫病が収まったので、素盞嗚神社の分霊を勧請し祀ったと社伝にあるhttps://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E8%83%BD%E8%A1%A8%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/@34.5573796,133.319335,17z/data=!4m5!3m4!1s0x35511923f416baa7:0xe94ea88b71fa02b0!8m2!3d34.5575979!4d133.3211035?hl=ja。 == 主な祭事 == === 祇園祭 === 祇園祭（現地では「祇園さん」・「天王さん」と呼ぶ）は、福山市の新市（しんいち）地区・戸手（とで）地区と相方（さがた）地区、[[府中市 (広島県)|府中市]]の中須（なかず）地区の4地区の氏子により年に1度、7月に行われる。 現在では戸手と相方が合同となり、3基の神輿で「神輿合わせ（喧嘩神輿）」が行われる。 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === * [[広島県]][[福山市]]新市町大字戸手1-1 === 交通アクセス === ; {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[福塩線]] [[上戸手駅]] （徒歩3分） ; {{underline|バス}} * [[福山駅]]（JR西日本[[山陽新幹線]]ほか）から、[[中国バス]]（府中・[[東城]]方面行き）で、「寺岡記念病院前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車 （下車後徒歩5分） ** ただし、祇園祭最終日19時からは交通規制により、バスの運行ルートが変更されるので注意が必要。 ; {{underline|自家用車}} * 祇園祭期間中は混雑し、自家用車での参拝は極めて困難。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[吉備津神社 (福山市)|吉備津神社]] （福山市新市町宮内） - 一般に備後国一宮とされる神社 * [[祇園信仰]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Susanoo-jinja_(Fukuyama)}} * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 素盞嗚神社]（新市町観光協会ホームページ） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すさのおしんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:福山市の歴史]] [[Category:福山市の建築物]] [[Category:備後国の式内社]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:一宮|加]] [[Category:備後国|社1すさのお]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:深津郡|社すさのお]] {{Pref-stub|pref=広島県}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[en:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[Category:Removed translated page templates]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[en:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is classified as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] }} [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] }} [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}}

According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] }} [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Susanoo-jinja (Fukuyama)]][[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[:en:Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|East Asian hip-and-gable roof]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] }} [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}}

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] }} [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)]] {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|version=102030047|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]]

A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) [[:en:Bingo Province|Ichinomiya]]
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally [[:en:673|673]]–[[:en:686|686]] (reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]]) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]]. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] in the 7th century (around [[:en:679|679]]). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from [[:en:Bingo Province|Bingo Province]] to [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:734|734]]. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[:en:Fukuen Line|Fukuen Line]]. * '''Bus:''' From [[:en:Fukuyama Station|Fukuyama Station]], take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[sn:usanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]] [[ja:素盞嗚神社 (福山市)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
