'''Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bingo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)|lt=Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|commons|Category:Susanoo-jinja (Fukuyama)|WD=Q11605639}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Susanowonono Shrine|lt=Susanowonono Shrine|WD=Q135041241}}; {{ill|Susanowonono Shrine|lt=Susanowonono Shrine|WD=Q135041241}} || {{coord|34.552739|133.279191|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183105 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Susanoo-jinja (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Susanoo-jinja (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Susanoo-jinja (Fukuyama)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fukuyama, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bingo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
'''Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bingo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)|lt=Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|commons|Category:Susanoo-jinja (Fukuyama)|WD=Q11605639}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Susanowonono Shrine|lt=Susanowonono Shrine|WD=Q135041241}}; {{ill|Susanowonono Shrine|lt=Susanowonono Shrine|WD=Q135041241}} || {{coord|34.552739|133.279191|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183105 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11605639}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Bingo Province}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679|1=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734|1=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station|1=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679|1=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734|1=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''

According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station|1=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Bingo Province}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679|1=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734|1=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station|1=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station|1=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
Torii, Zuishin Gate, and Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34|33|9.6|N|133|16|45.6|E|display=title,inline}} |deity = [[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] |status = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
(Claimed) {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Bingo Province}}
Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679|1=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734|1=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station|1=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]

Former [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] |established = Traditionally {{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}–{{ill|686|en|686|WD=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}} (reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}) |main_building = [[Irimoya-zukuri]] |festivals = [[Gion Matsuri]] (July 15) }} '''Susanoo Shrine''' (素盞嗚神社, ''Susanoo Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Shin'ichi Town, Fukuyama City|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinaisha]] and claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}. The shrine was formerly designated as a [[Kensha|Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. Known for its [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] and as the origin of the [[Gion Matsuri]], the shrine's grounds are said to have been the site of the mansion of the mythical giant [[Somin Shorai]]. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Susanoo no Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]''' (素盞嗚尊) ; Associated Deities * '''[[Kushinadahime|Inadahime no Mikoto]]''' (稲田姫命) – Consort of Susanoo * '''[[:en:Hachioji|Yahashira no Mikogami]]''' (八柱御子) – Offspring of Susanoo == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} in the 7th century (around {{ill|679|en|679|WD=Q3599607|lt=679|1=679}}). Later, the [[Tang dynasty]] envoy [[Kibi no Makibi]] is said to have brought the deity from {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|WD=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} to {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}’s [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|734|en|734|WD=Q23573|lt=734|1=734}}. The shrine is mentioned in a quote from the now-lost ''[[Bingo no Kuni Fudoki]]'' (circa Nara period) within ''[[Shaku Nihongi]]'' (compiled in the Kamakura period), as "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" (疫隈國社), though some believe this refers to its auxiliary shrine, Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine. It is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' as "Susanoo-no-Mikoto Shrine" in the ''Shikinaisha'' of [[Fukatsu District, Bingo Province|Fukatsu District, Bingo Province]]. During the [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine adopted [[Gozu Tennō]] as its deity and was renamed "Hayasayama Tenryūin Tennōji." The main hall (*hondō*), known as the Honchi Hall (now Tenmangū), housed the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} [[Kannon]] as its main deity, flanked by [[Acala]] and [[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the [[Shinbutsu bunri|separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the shrine reverted to its original deity, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, and was renamed to its current designation. == Grounds == ; Main Hall * [[File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 本殿.JPG|thumb|200px|Main Hall]] * Built in [[irimoya-zukuri]] style with a cypress bark roof. It is said to have been reconstructed by [[Mizuno Katsunari]], the first lord of Bingo Fukuyama Domain. ; '''Heiden''' (Offering Hall) * Connects the main hall and the worship hall. ; '''Naki Ryū''' ("Crying Dragon") * A rare acoustic feature shared by only about 20 buildings in Japan. ; Sacred Cedar ("Jindai no Sugi") * Once stood at the northeast corner of the grounds as a sacred tree. A stone monument marks its location. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 境内.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:素盞嗚神社神楽殿 (福山市新市町戸手).jpg|''Kagura Hall''
Hosts mikoshi for the Gion Matsuri. File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall (*Haiden*) File:素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手) 随神門.JPG|''Zuishin Gate'' == Subsidiary Shrines == ; Somin Shrine and Hōsō Shrine [[File:蘇民神社 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Somin and Hōsō Shrines]] * Located south of the main hall, these are considered to be the original "Enokuma Kuni-tsu Yashiro" of the ''Bingo no Kuni Fudoki.'' According to legend, the shrine sits on the site of the giant [[Somin Shorai]]’s mansion. ; Tode Tenmangū [[File:戸手天満宮 (素盞嗚神社 福山市新市町戸手 境内社).JPG|thumb|200px|Tode Tenmangū]] * Originally the Honchi Hall of the temple "Tenryūin Tennōji," it was converted into a Tenmangū during the [[Meiji period]]. == Festivals == ; Gion Matsuri The Gion Matsuri, locally referred to as "Gion-san" or "Tenno-san," is an annual festival held in July, featuring mikoshi processions from nearby communities. The festival includes "Mikoshi-Awase," a vigorous display of mikoshi parading. == Access == * '''Train:''' 3-minute walk from [[Kamitoide Station]] on the [[JR West]] {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|WD=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}. * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Fukuyama Station|en|Fukuyama Station|WD=Q800797|lt=Fukuyama Station|1=Fukuyama Station}}, take a [[Chugoku Bus]] to "Teraoka Memorial Hospital," then walk 5 minutes. == See Also == * [[Gion Faith|Gion faith]] * [[Kibitsu Shrine]] in Fukuyama, also considered Bingo Province's Ichinomiya. == External Links == * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=7 Susanoo Shrine] (Shin'ichi Town Tourism Association) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市)|version=43579126|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Susanoo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Susanoo Shrines 02.JPG|270px|Susanoo Shrine Front]]
